title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) prime?
generalization_signal: The six-class Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line now survives the first zero-slack p = 13 checkpoint with the unrefined C = 1 and {2,3}-smooth support-template: A ∪ E can still be filled by the full 24-label nontrivial {2,3}-smooth reservoir up to 193, while B ∪ F can be packed into a coprime-to-6 complement block. This materially upgrades the campaign from paired verified p = 11 feeders plus a four-class p = 13 repair to paired verified p = 13 feeders on both active zero-divisor lines.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) survives skeptical verification. The campaign-relevant gain is not just another exact feeder: it shows the six-class Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line does not break at the first zero-slack p = 13 boundary, so the current paired support-template program now has verified p = 13 feeders on both active zero-divisor lines. What still remains is to formalize the family support decompositions from the ring law and to package the arithmetic story as a family theorem rather than a feeder cluster.
candidate_theorem_slice: A support-template sufficient-condition theorem for odd primes p on the six-class Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line: after fixing the singleton class C to label 1, if {1, ..., p^2 + 2p - 2} contains 2(p - 1) labels for A ∪ E supported on a fixed small prime set S and another 2(p - 1) labels for B ∪ F avoiding every prime in S, then Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) is prime. The verified p = 13 feeder shows this remains honest even at the first zero-slack {2,3}-smooth boundary.
next_action: Update the zero-divisor family dossier around the paired verified p = 13 feeders and the new Lean support-decomposition surface, then move to Γ(Z_17 × Z_25) and the missing family bridge lemmas.
